properties.headline: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 23 at 3:54PM MDT until July 23 at 4:00PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
The storm which prompted the warning has weakened below severe
limits, and has exited the warned area. Therefore, the warning will
be allowed to expire.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 23 at 3:52PM MDT until July 23 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall over the Gold Mountain Fire Burn Scar
is possible.

* WHERE...Gold Mountain Fire Burn Scar in Ouray County Colorado,
including Dexter and Cutler Creek drainages and Highway 550.

* WHEN...Until 700 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Excessive rainfall is possible over the Gold Mountain
Burn Scar which could lead to flash flooding and/or debris flows,
particularly along highway 550 between Cutler Creek and Dexter
Creek. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 346 PM MDT, the probability of heavy rainfall will
increase through at least 7 pm MDT. If thunderstorms develop
over the Gold Mountain burn scar, debris flows are likely
within Cutler and Dexter Creek drainages, including Highway
550.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 23 at 3:49PM MDT until July 23 at 4:30PM MDT by NWS Goodland KS
properties.description:
SVRGLD

The National Weather Service in Goodland has issued a

* Severe Thunderstorm Warning for...
Southwestern Kit Carson County in east central Colorado...

* Until 430 PM MDT.

* At 348 PM MDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 4 miles west of
Flagler, moving southeast at 25 mph.

HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage
to roofs, siding, and trees.

* This severe thunderstorm will be near...
Flagler around 355 PM MDT.

This includes Interstate 70 in Colorado between mile markers 391 and
410.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 23 at 3:40PM MDT until July 23 at 6:15PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 340 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing heavy
rain over the Middle and South Aspen Acres Burn Scar. Up to 0.6
inches of rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 2 inches in
1 hour. Additional rainfall amounts up to 1 inch are possible in the
warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.
Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flow. The
debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose
materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms producing
flash flooding in and around the Middle and South Aspen
Acres Burn Scar.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around the
Middle and South Aspen Acres Burn Scar.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Rye, San Isabel, Beulah and Colorado City.

This includes the following high risk locations...
Beulah Water Intake.

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Saint Charles River, Muddy Creek and its tributaries, Spring Branch
and South Creek and its tributaries.

properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 23 at 3:19PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 318 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 7
miles south of Two Buttes Reservoir, or 8 miles north of Springfield,
moving east at 25 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts of 50 to 55 mph and nickel size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Two Buttes and Two Buttes Reservoir.
properties.areaDesc: Lamar Vicinity/Prowers County; Springfield Vicinity/Baca County
